content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5600                                322-5901                    322-6441

June 15, 1976                          A-76-300

Mr. Charles J. Nelson
Secretary, Board of Trustees
Gridley Union High School District
300 East Spruce Street
Gridley, California 95948

Dear Mr. Nelson:

A copy of the resolution adopted by the Board of Trustees of
the Gridley Union High School District on May 20 asking that
school board members be exempted from the disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act has been referred to this office
by the Office of the Governor.

If the Board of Trustees of the Gridley Union High School District
or members of its staff have any questions concerning the conflict
of interest codes which must be adopted in compliance with the
Political Reform Act, I suggest you contact Mr. Delbert Spurlock,
chief of the Conflict of Interest Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission, as soon as possible.

Mr. Spurlock and members of his staff can provide model codes
and other guidance in the preparation of the required codes.
They may also be able to relieve the Board of Trustees of its
concerns regarding the disclosure of certain economic interests.
The Conflict of Interest Division may be contacted by calling
916/322-6444.

If I may be of further assistance, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

Sincerely,

JOHN KEPLINGER, Chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

JK:mak
cc: Peter Sly, Governor's Office
    Delbert Spurlock

bcc: Jeanne[/]

Gridley Union High School

306 E. SPRUCE STREET
GRIDLEY, CALIFORNIA 95948

Phone 5638

RESOLUTION TO EXEMPT BOARDS OF EDUCATION OF
CALIFORNIA FROM THE PROVISIONS OF PROPOSITION
NINE, RELATING TO THE REQUIREMENT FOR FILING
EGONOMIC INTEREST STATEMENTS

WHEREAS the State of California has enacted legislation generally referred
to as the "Conflict of Interest Code" (Government Code 87300 et. seq.)
requiring all Board of Education members of the State to supply certain
information relative to their personal income and possessions, (such infor-
mation to become a matter of public record); and

WHEREAS the information required to be supplied by Board of Education
members will have only tenuous and infrequent relation to the expenditure
of public school funds by school districts; and

WHEREAS the public interest would be better served by the filing of sworn
statements by individual board members who may have a potential conflict
of interest in a specific fiscal matter; and

WHEREAS the requirements of Proposition Nine tend to demean, and question
the integrity of, citizens who as a civic contribution serve as unpaid members
of Boards of Education, thus tending to discourage citizens from offering
themselves for such service: THEREFORE BE IT

RESOLVED, That the Gridley Union High School District Board of Education
go on record as requesting the State Legislature and the Governor of the
State of California to exempt by legislation members of Boards of Education
from the provisions of the above-referred-to legislation; and be it

RESOLVED FURTHER, that copies of this Resolution be sent to the Butte
County Board of Supervisors, Butte County Counsel, Senator Randolph
Collier, Assemblyman Eugene Chappie, Assemblyman Pauline Davis,
California School Boards Association, Butte County Board of Education,
and all district Boards of Education of Butte County.

I, CHARLES J. NELSON, Secretary of the Board of Education of Gridley
Union High School District, Butte County, State of California, do hereby
certify the foregoing to be a true and correct copy of a resolution adopted
by the Governing Body of the aforesaid school district at a regular meeting
held on the twentieth (20th) day of May, 1976, as the same appears on record
in my office.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand this twenty first (21st)
day of May, 1976.

Charles J. Nelson, Secretary
Board of Trustees,
Gridley Union High School District

Gridley Union High School
300 E. SPRUCE STREET
GRIDLEY CALIFORNIA 95948
Phone 5636

May 25, 1976

The Honorable Edmund G. Brown, Jr.,
Governor, State of California,
State Capitol Building,
Capitol Mall,
Sacramento, California

Dear Governor Brown:

At the regular meeting of the Board of Trustees of the
Gridley Union High School District held May 20, 1976, the
enclosed resolution was adopted.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Charles J. Nelson
Dist. Supt./Principal

sections.question_synthetic: Should the Gridley Union High School District be exempted from the disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (Proposition 9) regarding economic interest statements for school board members?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC does not grant the exemption request. Instead, the Chief of the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division directs the District to contact the Conflict of Interest Division to discuss model codes and potential relief regarding disclosure concerns through proper conflict of interest code adoption.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains FPPC response letter (June 15, 1976) from John Keplinger followed by supporting materials including the original resolution request and transmittal form. No formal Q/C sections present; conclusion is implicit in the referral guidance provided rather than explicitly stated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Should the Gridley Union High School District be exempted from the disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (Proposition 9) regarding economic interest statements for school board members?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC does not grant the exemption request. Instead, the Chief of the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division directs the District to contact the Conflict of Interest Division to discuss model codes and potential relief regarding disclosure concerns through proper conflict of interest code adoption.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a school district's request for exemption from Proposition 9 disclosure requirements by declining to exempt board members and instead referring the district to the Conflict of Interest Division for guidance on adopting required conflict of interest codes that may address their concerns.
